message:
perf(evaluator): eliminate duplicate normalize_command calls

Added pack_aware_quick_reject_with_normalized() which returns both the
quick-reject decision and the normalized command. This allows the caller
to reuse the normalized form for pack evaluation instead of calling
normalize_command again.

Updated evaluate_command and evaluate_command_legacy_and_packs to use
the optimized function and reuse the normalized command for:
- Allowlist matching
- Pack pattern evaluation

This reduces normalization from 2-3 calls per evaluation to 1 call.

Closes git_safety_guard-ksk.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4","title":"Simulate: fixtures + integration tests + detailed logging","description":"## Objective

Add tests that make `dcg simulate` trustworthy and easy to evolve.

## Test strategy

### Unit tests

- parser unit tests (format detection) live with `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1`.

### Integration tests (this task)

- Run the compiled binary with `dcg simulate` against fixture log files.
- Assert:
  - exit codes
  - key summary counts
  - top rule ordering stability
  - redaction/truncation behavior

## Logging requirements

Test failures should print:

- which fixture file
- which line number caused parse failure
- the offending line (truncated)
- the expected vs actual summary

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests are deterministic across runs.
- Adding a new fixture regression is easy.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3` (output)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","title":"[PACK] remote.ssh - SSH Pack","description":"# SSH Remote Access Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for SSH operations, particularly remote command execution.

## Destructive Patterns
### Remote Command Execution
- `ssh host 'dangerous-command'` - Detect destructive commands executed remotely
- Piping destructive scripts through SSH
- Redirecting scripts into SSH

### Key Management
- `ssh-keygen -R host` - Removes known hosts (security implication)
- Deleting SSH keys

### Config Operations
- Modifying ~/.ssh/config destructively
- Removing authorized_keys entries

## Safe Patterns
- `ssh -V` (version)
- `ssh-keygen -l` (fingerprint display)
- `ssh-keyscan` (key scanning)
- `ssh host` (interactive session - user takes responsibility)
- `ssh host 'safe-command'` (non-destructive remote commands)

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: remote.* (git_safety_guard-g5o)
- Keywords: ssh, ssh-keygen
- CRITICAL: Must detect when OTHER destructive commands are tunneled through SSH

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for remote delete commands via SSH
- [ ] Unit tests for piped script detection
- [ ] Unit tests for key management operations
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/remote_ssh.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Remote destructive commands detected
- [ ] Key removal operations blocked
- [ ] Safe SSH usage allowed
- [ ] Interactive sessions not blocked","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:13.436955387-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:00:07.922675119-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T03:00:07.922675119-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented remote.ssh pack with 6 destructive patterns and 10 safe patterns; all 8 tests pass","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.132964887-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.177154661-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.223350479-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.270713954-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.316989212-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.363582267-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.365141001-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.41138624-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1uf","title":"E2E: path normalization + quoting/encoding matrix","description":"## Objective

Expand E2E coverage around tricky path/quoting scenarios that historically cause false positives/negatives.

## Scope

- Absolute paths (`/usr/bin/git`, `/bin/rm`) plus quoted command words.
- Mixed quoting, escaped characters, Unicode edge cases, env assignments, and sudo/env wrappers.
- Encode tests for command substitution and pipeline contexts.

## Acceptance criteria

- New matrix cases added to E2E suite with clear expected results.
- Failures are easy to diagnose from logs.

- `fastly domain list`
- `fastly version list`

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-23g","title":"[PACK] backup.restic - Restic Pack","description":"# Restic Backup Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Restic backup operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `restic forget` - Removes snapshots
- `restic forget --prune` - Removes + reclaims space
- `restic prune` - Removes unreferenced data
- `restic key remove` - Removes encryption keys
- `restic unlock --remove-all` - Force removes locks
- `restic cache --cleanup`

## Safe Patterns
- `restic snapshots`
- `restic ls`
- `restic stats`
- `restic check`
- `restic diff`
- `restic find`
- `restic backup` (additive)
- `restic restore`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: backup.* (git_safety_guard-alc)
- Keywords: restic
- Note: forget without --keep-* flags is extremely dangerous

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Snapshot removal blocked
- [ ] Prune operations blocked
- [ ] Key removal blocked
- [ ] Backup/restore allowed
- [ ] Tests cover common Restic operations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:32:50.784932143-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:39:02.206599212-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:39:02.206599212-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.390900999-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.43091172-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.470796614-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.510737012-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.55195793-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.591207458-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.746487597-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.792227725-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-26f","title":"Define TypeScript destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"## Objective

Define TypeScript heredoc patterns with a focus on **runtime destructive operations**, while avoiding low-signal “TypeScript style” heuristics that would create false positives.

## Scope boundary

TypeScript compiles to JavaScript; destructive operations are the same at runtime.

Therefore:

- Reuse the JavaScript/Node pattern families from `git_safety_guard-mcf`.
- Add only TypeScript-syntax coverage (types, generics, decorators) so the parser can still match the same runtime calls.

## Pattern families (v1)

- Recursive filesystem deletion:
  - `fs.rmSync(..., { recursive: true })`
  - `fs.rmdirSync(..., { recursive: true })`
- Process execution with destructive payload:
  - `child_process.execSync("rm -rf ...")`
  - `spawnSync("rm", ["-rf", ...])`

## Explicitly out of scope (avoid FPs)

- Heuristics like “@ts-ignore preceding dangerous code”
- “any type casts” / “type assertions” as signals

These are too common and do not map directly to destructive operations.

## Acceptance Criteria

- TS patterns are a superset of JS patterns in terms of runtime destructive operations, differing only in syntax support.
- Includes positive + negative fixtures that exercise TS syntax (types/generics/decorators) without changing runtime behavior.
- No default hard denies unless the matched operation is clearly catastrophic.
","notes":"Taking this next to unblock git_safety_guard-e7m: expand TypeScript heredoc patterns (mirror JS families) + add TS-syntax-focused fixtures; reuse/refine catastrophic-path and destructive-literal payload detection; run full gates + push.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonFalcon","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:17.541114003-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T06:09:08.474522952-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T06:09:08.474522952-05:00","close_reason":"Expanded TypeScript heredoc patterns to mirror JS families (fs rm/rmdir + execSync/spawnSync + Deno.remove), with refinement to block only on catastrophic literal paths and destructive literal shell payloads. Added ≥5 positive + ≥5 negative TS fixtures covering TS syntax (type assertions, generics, decorators). Full fmt/check/clippy/test + e2e pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-26f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:34.25492655-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-26f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:34.273731104-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-26f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-mcf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:34.292923988-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-26f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:44.680006442-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-2cu","title":"Design false positive reduction strategy for string arguments","description":"## Objective

Design and implement strategies to dramatically reduce false positives in command blocking, especially for documentation and string arguments.

## The Core Problem

The current regex-based guard suffers from context blindness. It sees:
```
bd create --description="Pattern blocks rm -rf commands"
```

And matches "rm -rf" without understanding it's:
1. Inside a quoted string
2. An argument to a documentation tool
3. NOT actually being executed

This creates severe workflow disruption when trying to document the very patterns we're blocking.

## Why This Is Critical

False positives are arguably WORSE than false negatives:
- False negative: A dangerous command might slip through (rare, recoverable with backups)
- False positive: Blocks legitimate work, forces workarounds, erodes trust in the tool
- Repeated false positives lead users to disable the guard entirely

The guard must be TRUSTED to make intelligent decisions, not blindly pattern-match text.

## Solution Approaches

### 1. Command Structure Analysis (Primary Approach)

Parse the top-level command with a lightweight shell/token model to understand structure:
- Identify command name (bd, git, echo, etc.)
- Identify argument positions (which are options vs values)
- Identify quoting context (single, double, unquoted)
- Only apply destructive patterns to EXECUTABLE positions

Example analysis:
```
bd create --title="..." --description="rm -rf pattern docs"
         ^command       ^option         ^string value (NOT executed)
```

### 2. Safe Command Registry

Maintain a list of commands that take non-executable string arguments:
- bd create, bd update (--description, --title)
- git commit (-m), git tag (-m)
- echo, printf (arguments are printed, not executed)
- grep, rg (pattern arguments)

For these commands, don't apply destructive patterns to their string arguments.

### 3. Execution Context Detection

Distinguish between:
- **Direct execution**: The string IS the command (`bash dangerous_cmd`)
- **String literal**: The string is DATA passed to a command (`bd --desc="..."`)
- **Heredoc body**: Requires language-specific analysis (the whole point of ast-grep)

### 4. Two-Phase Analysis

1. **Quick structural check**: parse command structure, identify context
2. **Pattern matching**: only apply patterns to executable contexts
3. **Deep analysis**: for heredocs/complex cases, use ast-grep / language-aware scanning

### 5. Confidence Scoring (optional later)

Instead of binary block/allow:
- High confidence dangerous: block immediately
- Medium confidence: more thorough analysis
- Low confidence (looks like documentation): allow with optional warning

## Implementation Strategy

1. Choose the minimal parsing substrate that meets performance needs:
   - tokenization + quoting + operator detection (pipes/subst)
   - optionally tree-sitter-bash if needed for correctness
2. Define `ExecutionContext` enum: Direct, StringArg, Heredoc, PipeTarget, CommandSubst
3. Maintain a Safe String-Argument Registry (command + flags) and apply it only in `StringArg` context.
4. Ensure ambiguity defaults to safe behavior (do not “trust” unknown flags as safe).

## Test Cases

Essential false positive tests:
- `bd create --description="This blocks rm -rf"` → ALLOW
- `git commit -m "Fix rm -rf pattern matching"` → ALLOW
- `echo "example: git reset --hard"` → ALLOW
- `grep "rm -rf" patterns.txt` → ALLOW

Essential true positive tests (should still block):
- `rm -rf /tmp/*` → BLOCK
- `bash -c "rm -rf /"` → BLOCK
- `python3 << 'EOF'
  import os; os.system("rm -rf /")
  EOF` → BLOCK (via heredoc analysis)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Design is captured in a self-contained doc/ADR-like section (does not require reading external plan docs).
- Defines:
  - the execution-context model
  - the initial Safe String-Argument Registry scope
  - conservative default rules for ambiguity
- Provides a concrete test matrix of:
  - must-allow doc/test cases
  - must-block executed cases
- Specifies a performance budget for the parsing/classification layer (initial target: sub-5ms worst-case, revise after baseline).
","notes":"Design document complete: docs/design-2cu-false-positive-reduction.md - Formalizes execution context model, SafeStringRegistry scope, test matrix, performance budget. Key finding: context.rs implementation is solid, needs evaluator integration.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:36:56.104462373-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T01:43:55.48241764-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T01:43:55.48241764-05:00","close_reason":"Design complete. Document at docs/design-2cu-false-positive-reduction.md covers execution context model, SafeStringRegistry scope, test matrix, performance budget. Key finding: context.rs already has solid implementation, needs evaluator.rs integration.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-2cu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-b45","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:02.996636534-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-2cu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-2j3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:03.01736459-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-2cu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:02:17.777570479-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -182,7 +182,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","title":"Implement false positive reduction for string arguments","description":"# False Positive Reduction Implementation

## Problem

Current regex patterns match dangerous strings even in safe contexts:
- `bd create --description="This blocks rm -rf"` → BLOCKED (wrong!)
- `git commit -m "Fix rm -rf pattern"` → BLOCKED (wrong!)
- `grep "rm -rf" patterns.txt` → BLOCKED (wrong!)

## Solution

Parse command structure to identify execution context before applying destructive patterns.

## Implementation

### 1. Safe Command Registry

```rust
static SAFE_STRING_ARG_COMMANDS: &[(&str, &[&str])] = &[
    ("bd", &["--description", "--title", "-d", "-t"]),
    ("git", &["commit -m", "tag -m", "notes add -m"]),
    ("grep", &["-e", "--regexp"]),
    ("rg", &["-e", "--regexp"]),
    ("echo", &[]),  // all args are strings
    ("printf", &[]),
];
```

### 2. Execution Context Detection

```rust
enum ExecutionContext {
    Direct,           // Command itself is executed
    StringArgument,   // Inside quoted string argument to safe command
    HeredocBody,      // Inside heredoc (needs Tier 2 analysis)
    PipeTarget,       // Right side of pipe (executed)
    CommandSubst,     // Inside $() or backticks (executed)
}

fn detect_execution_context(cmd: &str, position: usize) -> ExecutionContext;
```

### 3. Integration Point

Modify main check flow:

```rust
fn check_command(cmd: &str) -> CheckResult {
    // Quick reject (existing)
    if global_quick_reject(cmd) {
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // NEW: Check if dangerous patterns are in safe context
    if is_safe_string_context(cmd) {
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // Continue with heredoc detection and pattern matching...
}
```

## Test Cases

### Must ALLOW (false positive prevention)
- `bd create --description="Pattern blocks rm -rf commands"`
- `git commit -m "Fix git reset --hard detection"`
- `grep "rm -rf" src/patterns.rs`
- `echo "example: git push --force"`
- `rg -e "DROP TABLE" --type sql`

### Must BLOCK (true positives preserved)
- `rm -rf /tmp/*`
- `git reset --hard HEAD~5`
- `bash -c "rm -rf /"`
- `echo "hi" | bash -c "rm -rf /"`

## Performance

- < 100 microseconds additional overhead
- Simple string prefix matching for command detection
- No regex needed for this layer

## Dependencies

- Depends on: False positive design (2cu)

## Acceptance Criteria

- The documented false-positive examples are allowed in hook mode and `dcg test`.
- True-positive executions are still blocked, including destructive strings inside:
  - `bash -c ...`
  - `sh -c ...`
  - `node -e ...` (where the code executes)
  - command substitution (`$(...)` / backticks)
- Context classification is conservative:
  - only skips matching inside explicitly-registered safe string arguments
  - does not treat unknown flags/commands as safe
- Performance overhead is bounded (target < 100µs additional overhead on typical allow-path commands).
","notes":"## Objective

Implement the execution-context + safe-string-arg behavior described in `git_safety_guard-t8x` so dcg stops blocking documentation/testing workflows (commit messages, bd descriptions, grep/rg patterns) while still blocking real executions.

## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","title":"Implement docker-compose extractor","description":"## Objective

Implement an extractor for docker-compose files to detect executable command contexts.

## File Pattern

- `docker-compose.yml`
- `docker-compose.yaml`
- `compose.yml`
- `compose.yaml`

## Extraction Targets

### Command Blocks (executed)
- `command:` values
- `entrypoint:` values
- `healthcheck.test:` commands

### NOT Extracted (data only)
- `environment:` values
- `labels:` values
- Volume paths
- Comments

## Example

```yaml
services:
  app:
    command: /bin/sh -c "rm -rf /data/*"  # EXTRACT THIS
    environment:
      CLEANUP: "rm -rf /"  # DO NOT extract
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Correctly extracts command/entrypoint with line numbers
- Handles both string and array formats
- Ignores environment variables and labels
- Unit tests with representative fixtures","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Extract only from executed compose fields (e.g., `command`, `entrypoint`) and avoid scanning:
  - environment variables
  - comments
  - labels/metadata
- Include negative fixtures for dangerous strings in env/labels.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:57.298774964-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T13:11:56.093978239-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T13:11:56.093978239-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: docker-compose extractor with 9 tests. Extracts command/entrypoint/healthcheck.test, correctly ignores environment/labels/volumes/ports. 701 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:20.580453194-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:46:02.44142759-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-fuzz","title":"Implement fuzzing and property-based testing for heredoc parser","description":"# Fuzzing and Property-Based Testing

## Objective

Ensure heredoc detection is robust against malformed, malicious, or unexpected input through automated testing.

## Fuzzing with cargo-fuzz

### Setup

```toml
# Cargo.toml
[dev-dependencies]
libfuzzer-sys = "0.4"
arbitrary = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
```

### Fuzz Targets

```rust
// fuzz/fuzz_targets/heredoc_extraction.rs
#![no_main]
use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;

fuzz_target!(|data: &str| {
    // Should never panic
    let _ = extract_heredoc(data);
});

// fuzz/fuzz_targets/language_detection.rs
fuzz_target!(|data: &str| {
    let _ = detect_language(data);
});

// fuzz/fuzz_targets/tier1_regex.rs
fuzz_target!(|data: &str| {
    let _ = might_contain_heredoc(data);
});

// fuzz/fuzz_targets/full_pipeline.rs
fuzz_target!(|data: &str| {
    // Full check should never panic
    let _ = check_command(data);
});
```

### Running fuzzing

```bash
cargo install cargo-fuzz
cargo +nightly fuzz run heredoc_extraction -- -max_len=100000
cargo +nightly fuzz run full_pipeline -- -max_total_time=3600
```

## Property-Based Testing with proptest

```toml
[dev-dependencies]
proptest = "1.4"
```

## Structured fuzzing

Use `arbitrary` to generate structured heredoc inputs with variants (standard/indented/quoted).

## CI Integration

Run a bounded fuzz budget on a schedule (weekly) and allow manual trigger.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Fuzz targets exist for at least:
  - tier1 trigger
  - heredoc extraction
  - language detection
  - full pipeline
- Fuzzing runs for a bounded time in CI (scheduled) and produces reproducible crash artifacts.
- Property tests encode key invariants:
  - no panics
  - tier1 trigger is a superset of tier2 extraction triggers (no false negatives)
  - determinism (same input → same decision) where applicable
- Any discovered crash is converted into a minimal repro regression test.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","title":"[CATEGORY] remote.* - Remote Access Pack Suite","description":"# Remote Access Pack Suite

## Overview
Remote access tools (SSH, scp, rsync) can execute commands and transfer files to remote systems. Accidental destructive commands on production servers are common footguns.

## Packs to Implement

### remote.ssh
- **Why Critical**: SSH provides direct access to remote systems. Commands executed remotely have same risks as local.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `ssh host 'destructive-command'` - Remote command execution
  - Piping destructive scripts through SSH
  - `ssh-keygen -R` - Removes known hosts (security)
  - Key deletion operations
- **Safe Patterns**: `ssh -V`, `ssh-keygen -l` (fingerprint), connection testing

### remote.scp
- **Why Critical**: scp can overwrite remote files. Wrong direction can overwrite source or dest.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - Overwriting critical remote paths
  - Recursive copy to wrong destination
- **Safe Patterns**: Read-only operations, specific file copies

### remote.rsync
- **Why Critical**: rsync --delete removes files at destination not in source. Common footgun.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `rsync --delete` - Deletes extra files at dest
  - `rsync --delete-before` / `--delete-after`
  - Wrong source/dest direction
- **Safe Patterns**: `--dry-run`, `--list-only`, `-n`

## Special Note
These commands are meta-dangerous because they can execute ANY command remotely. Pattern matching should detect when destructive commands are being tunneled through SSH.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 3 Epic (git_safety_guard-9ic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSH remote command execution analyzed
- [ ] rsync --delete variants detected
- [ ] Dry-run flags recognized as safe
- [ ] Tests cover remote execution patterns","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:29:31.744652142-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:29:31.744652142-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-78a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:35:10.544925551-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:01.156321085-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:01.190182549-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.208601095-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.254100032-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.302424925-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.349723839-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.395123369-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.439109319-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-gie","title":"Fix dcg: unknown CLI args should error (no stdin hang)","description":"## Problem

`src/main.rs` ignored clap parse errors. Running `dcg --unknown` (or any invalid CLI invocation) fell through to hook-mode and blocked on stdin, which looks like a hang and is confusing UX.

## Fix

- If CLI parsing fails, print the clap error and exit (status 2).
- Keep custom `--help/-h` and `--version/-V` output behavior.
- Hook mode (stdin JSON, no args) remains unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg --this-flag-does-not-exist` exits quickly with a clear error.
- `dcg <subcommand>` continues to work normally.
- Hook invocation via stdin JSON still works.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-08T04:20:37.767980269-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T04:21:49.547802511-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T04:21:49.547802511-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed main CLI dispatch: unknown args now print clap error and exit 2 (no stdin hang)."}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-goph","title":"Define Go destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"# Go Destructive Patterns for Heredoc Scanning

## Objective

Define Go heredoc matchers that detect **high-signal destructive operations**, primarily when Go code is used as a wrapper to execute destructive shell commands or delete recursively.

## Scope boundary

- Focus on irreversible filesystem/process operations.
- Do not turn this into a general Go security scanner (exclude unsafe/reflection/network heuristics).

## Pattern families (v1)

### 1) Process execution with destructive payload (high signal)

- `exec.Command("rm", "-rf", ...).Run()` (literal)
- `exec.Command("bash", "-c", "rm -rf ...")` (literal)
- `exec.Command("git", "reset", "--hard", ... )` (literal)

Avoid low-signal matches:

- `exec.Command($CMD, ...)` should not be a default deny.

### 2) Recursive deletion (high signal)

- `os.RemoveAll("/")` / other clearly catastrophic literal targets → Error
- `os.RemoveAll("./build")` → Warning at most

## False positive mitigation

- Require negative fixtures for:
  - benign command execution (`exec.Command("go", "test")`)
  - benign cleanup under `/tmp`
  - code that prints dangerous strings

## Acceptance Criteria

- Defines an initial Go pattern set focused on `exec.Command` + destructive payloads and `os.RemoveAll`.
- Includes at least 5 positive and 5 negative fixtures.
- No default hard denies unless the matched operation is clearly catastrophic.

Commit a0a89bd.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-goph","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:46:45.603432505-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-goph","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:51:56.680228592-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","title":"[CATEGORY] platform.gitlab - GitLab Platform Pack","description":"# GitLab Platform Pack

## Overview
GitLab provides integrated DevOps platform with CI/CD, container registry, and infrastructure management. Similar risks to GitHub but with additional features.

## Why This Pack
- GitLab CI/CD variables contain secrets
- Project deletion removes all associated data
- Runner tokens provide CI access
- Protected branch removal exposes main

## Key Destructive Patterns

### glab CLI
- `glab repo delete` - Repository deletion
- `glab variable delete` - Removes CI variables
- `glab release delete` - Deletes releases
- `glab api -X DELETE` - Raw API deletions

### API Patterns
- `DELETE /projects/{id}`
- `DELETE /projects/.../variables/`
- `DELETE /projects/.../hooks/`
- `DELETE /projects/.../protected_branches/`

### gitlab-rails (self-hosted)
- `gitlab-rails runner` with destructive code
- `gitlab-rake` maintenance tasks

## Safe Patterns
- `glab repo view`, `glab project list`
- `glab variable list`
- `glab api` with GET method

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 2 Epic (git_safety_guard-hhh)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] glab CLI patterns comprehensive
- [ ] API endpoint patterns detected
- [ ] Self-hosted admin patterns covered
- [ ] Tests cover common destructive operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:28:24.685736839-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:05:24.163371572-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:05:24.163371572-05:00","close_reason":"platform.gitlab pack implemented and registered, all dependencies closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-z2q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:30.648419203-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.214582738-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.261309686-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.308009552-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.354369499-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.40221398-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.449110616-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.5","title":"Eliminate duplicate normalization/classification","description":"Refactor evaluator flow to avoid repeated normalize + classify passes.","notes":"Currently we normalize/classify in quick-reject and later re-normalize for pack matching. Reuse the normalized/sanitized view when safe.

Invariant: if sanitize_for_pattern_matching returns Owned, the keyword gate must re-run on the sanitized form (current behavior). After that, only normalize once and reuse for pack checks.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.6","title":"Safe-pattern RegexSet fast path","description":"Use RegexSet to skip per-pattern safe checks when no safe pattern can match.","notes":"Approach: build a RegexSet for linear-safe patterns per pack (or per tier). If set misses, skip safe pattern checks. If set hits, fall back to ordered evaluation to preserve first-match semantics.

Isomorphism: set is only used as a negative filter; any potential match must still be validated by the original ordered logic.

Testing/Validation:
- Must pass golden corpus + shared e2e harness; targeted unit tests in ksk.6.2.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.692467974-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T17:04:02.979403016-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.693917067-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:28.250244892-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:58.546023653-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:21:01.818777732-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
